agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform feature that automatically generates and maintains optimized delivery routes for drivers throughout their shift, with real-time recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, and vehicle-class enforcement.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: Route Planning (optimization algorithm, recalculation triggers), Fleet Management (driver shifts, vehicle licensing, zone assignment), Delivery Lifecycle (delivery status, zone membership, failure handling), and Customer Notification (proximity-based alerts). The primary context under design is Route Planning, which consumes events from the other three.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize travel time/distance within their zone for a given shift."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area that scopes both driver assignment and delivery grouping. A driver operates within exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The act of recomputing the optimized route in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, or manual deviation)."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver physically departs from the system-suggested route sequence, detected by the system (e.g., visiting stops out of order or traveling off-path)."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A sliding window of the three upcoming delivery stops on the driver's active route, used to trigger customer notifications."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that constrains which deliveries a driver may be assigned based on shipment requirements."},{"term":"Delivery Stop","definition":"A single delivery location on a route, with an estimated service time (default 4 minutes, overridable per zone)."},{"term":"Shift Start","definition":"The event marking a driver's availability, which triggers initial route assignment."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, requiring removal from the active route and triggering recalculation."},{"term":"Estimated Stop Time","definition":"The configurable time budget per delivery stop, defaulting to 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically compute and assign an optimized route from all pending deliveries in their zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy — the business could choose manual assignment or delayed assignment as alternatives. It is not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Within [SLA TBD] seconds of a driver starting their shift, a route containing all pending zone deliveries (compatible with the driver's vehicle class) is assigned to them."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capability.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Assigning a motorcycle driver to a freight shipment is physically impossible and likely violates transport regulations. If this constraint is violated, the system is in a corrupt state — the delivery cannot be fulfilled and may create legal liability.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass <= driver.licensedVehicleClass. No route assignment or recalculation may produce a violation."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an optimization policy. The business could choose to batch recalculations or defer them. The system would not be corrupt without immediate recalculation.","testable_assertion":"When a new delivery enters a zone with an active driver route, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery within [SLA TBD]."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy for route efficiency. The system would not be corrupt if a failed delivery simply stayed on the route temporarily.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery status transitions to 'failed', it is removed from the active route and the remaining route is reoptimized."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational optimization policy. A stale route is suboptimal but does not corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"When the system detects that a driver has deviated from the planned route sequence, recalculation is triggered using the driver's current position as the new starting point."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification is a business policy, not a data integrity constraint. Missing a notification does not corrupt the domain.","testable_assertion":"When a route is calculated or recalculated, any customer whose delivery is now within the first 3 stops of the route (and was not previously notified) receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter, explicitly designed to be changed by operations staff.","testable_assertion":"Route time estimates use the zone-specific override if configured, otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. Only the operations team role can set overrides."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their designated zone for the current shift.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries define the operational domain of a driver. Cross-zone assignment would violate the fundamental partitioning model — the system cannot guarantee route quality, SLA compliance, or regulatory adherence if zone boundaries are ignored.","testable_assertion":"Every delivery on a driver's route must have delivery.zone == driver.currentShift.zone. No route assignment or recalculation may assign cross-zone deliveries."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift in zone A with 10 pending deliveries in zone A","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system assigns an optimized route containing all 10 deliveries, ordered to minimize travel time/distance, within the route calculation SLA"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver with an active route in zone A","when":"A new delivery is created in zone A","then":"The driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery, and any customers newly in the 'next 3 stops' window are notified"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver with an active route containing delivery D at stop 5","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"Delivery D is removed from the route, remaining stops are reoptimized, and the 'next 3 stops' window is re-evaluated for notifications"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver whose suggested next stop is location B","when":"The driver proceeds to location C (a later stop) instead","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver with a motorcycle license and an active route","when":"A freight-class delivery is added to their zone","then":"The freight delivery is NOT added to the motorcycle driver's route, even though it is in their zone"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer whose delivery is at position 4 on the route","when":"Route recalculation moves their delivery to position 3","then":"The customer receives a 'your delivery is approaching' notification"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer who was already notified (delivery was at position 2)","when":"Route recalculation keeps their delivery within the next 3 stops","then":"The customer is NOT notified again (no duplicate notifications)"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"Zone B has a configured stop time override of 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"Each delivery stop uses 6 minutes (not the default 4) for time estimation"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver in zone A with an active route","when":"A new delivery is created in zone B","then":"The delivery is NOT added to the zone A driver's route"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Multiple drivers are active in zone A","when":"A new delivery arrives in zone A","then":"The system assigns the delivery to the most optimal driver and recalculates only the affected driver's route"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A driver starts their shift but there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","expected_behavior":"The driver is assigned an empty route. When the first delivery arrives in their zone, recalculation triggers and assigns it."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require a vehicle class higher than the only active driver's license","expected_behavior":"The driver receives an empty route. Deliveries remain unassigned/pending. The system should alert operations that unserviceable deliveries exist."},{"scenario":"A route recalculation is triggered while a previous recalculation is still in progress","expected_behavior":"The system must handle concurrent triggers gracefully — either queue recalculations or cancel-and-restart with the latest state. The route must never reflect stale data."},{"scenario":"A driver deviates from the route to the location of an upcoming stop (skip-ahead)","expected_behavior":"The system detects deviation, confirms the driver is at a valid delivery stop, marks it as the current stop, and recalculates the remaining route from that position."},{"scenario":"A customer's delivery oscillates in and out of the 'next 3 stops' window across multiple rapid recalculations","expected_behavior":"The customer receives at most one notification. Once notified, the flag is set and persists regardless of subsequent recalculations."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone with multiple active drivers","expected_behavior":"The optimization must decide which driver gets the delivery based on route efficiency, not arbitrary assignment. This implies a cross-route optimization or assignment heuristic."},{"scenario":"The operations team changes the zone stop time override mid-shift while drivers have active routes","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full route reorder or only ETA updates needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A driver's shift ends but deliveries remain on their route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool and reassigned to the next available driver. They must not be silently dropped."},{"scenario":"A failed delivery is retried — does it re-enter the pending pool?","expected_behavior":"Business must define retry policy. If retried, the delivery re-enters the pending pool and triggers recalculation for an available driver. Retry count limits should apply."},{"scenario":"Driver completes all deliveries mid-shift and new deliveries arrive later","expected_behavior":"The driver should receive a recalculated route with the new deliveries, as they are still on shift and in-zone."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries per shift (throughput), and reduces overtime. Vehicle class enforcement avoids regulatory fines and failed delivery attempts. Accurate ETAs reduce missed-delivery costs.","operational":"Automatic route assignment eliminates manual dispatch overhead. Real-time recalculation keeps routes optimal as conditions change. Zone-level stop time overrides let operations fine-tune for local conditions (urban density, building access, etc.).","user":"Customer notifications at 'next 3 stops' improve delivery experience and reduce 'where is my package' inquiries. Drivers get clear, optimized routes reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue.","risk":"Route optimization algorithm quality is critical — a poor algorithm negates all benefits. Frequent recalculations could cause driver confusion or notification spam. Vehicle class enforcement failure creates legal/safety liability. Zone boundary accuracy is foundational — incorrect zones corrupt all routing.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Primary users. Route quality directly affects their workload, earnings (if performance-based), and job satisfaction. Excessive recalculations mid-drive could be disorienting or dangerous."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving visibility. Poor routing extends delivery windows and erodes trust."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration control. Reduced manual dispatch. Must monitor for unserviceable deliveries and driver-vehicle mismatches."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable improvement in deliveries-per-shift, fuel costs, and on-time delivery rates as KPIs."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement is a regulatory requirement. Audit trail of route assignments and vehicle class checks may be required."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec clearly defines the core triggers (shift start, new delivery, failed delivery, deviation) and the vehicle class constraint. However, multiple operational details are described vaguely or not at all: how deviation is detected, how multi-driver assignment works, what optimization objective is used, how zone boundaries are defined, and what happens at shift boundaries. These ambiguities affect the ability to build a correct implementation without making assumptions.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class enforcement is a hard invariant — clearly stated and unambiguous","Three recalculation triggers are well-defined events","Customer notification at 'next 3 stops' is a clear threshold","Default stop time of 4 minutes with zone-level override is explicit"],"low_confidence_areas":["How 'manual deviation' is detected — GPS threshold? Sequence violation? Driver-reported?","Multi-driver zone assignment strategy — how does the system choose which driver gets a new delivery?","Route optimization objective — minimize distance? Time? Balance load across drivers?","Shift lifecycle — what happens to route when shift ends, or during break?","Failed delivery retry policy — does it re-enter the pool? How many retries?","Zone definition and management — how are zones created, bounded, and updated?"],"assumptions":["A driver operates in exactly one zone per shift","Vehicle class is a hierarchical capability (van driver can handle motorcycle-class deliveries)","'Next 3 stops' notification is sent at most once per customer per delivery","Route recalculation replaces the entire remaining route, not just inserts/removes a single stop","The system has real-time or near-real-time driver location data for deviation detection"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection mechanism is unspecified. This could mean GPS-based off-path detection, visiting stops out of order, or driver self-reporting. Each interpretation produces fundamentally different system requirements (real-time GPS tracking vs. check-in sequence monitoring vs. UI interaction).","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Engineering","Operations Team"],"options":["GPS geofence-based: trigger when driver is >X meters from expected route for >Y seconds","Sequence-based: trigger when driver checks in at a stop that is not the next planned stop","Driver-initiated: driver taps 'reroute' in their app"],"recommendation":"Sequence-based detection is the most pragmatic starting point — it requires no continuous GPS processing and aligns with the delivery check-in flow. GPS-based can be layered on later."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver assignment within a zone is not addressed. When a new delivery enters a zone with multiple active drivers, the spec says 'recalculate the route' but does not specify WHICH driver's route, or whether cross-driver optimization occurs.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Customers"],"options":["Nearest-driver: assign to driver currently closest to the new delivery","Least-loaded: assign to driver with fewest remaining stops","Global reoptimization: reoptimize all drivers' routes in the zone simultaneously"],"recommendation":"Least-loaded with distance tiebreaker is the simplest fair heuristic. Global reoptimization is ideal but significantly more complex — recommend as a Phase 2 enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Route optimization objective function is unspecified. 'Optimized route' could mean minimize total distance, minimize total time, minimize latest delivery time, balance workload across drivers, or prioritize time-sensitive deliveries.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Finance/Management"],"options":["Minimize total travel distance (fuel-efficient)","Minimize total route time including stop times (throughput-maximizing)","Minimize maximum individual delivery ETA (customer-fairness-maximizing)"],"recommendation":"Minimize total route time is the most common industry default as it directly maximizes throughput. Customer-facing SLA constraints should be layered on as hard constraints, not the objective function."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform feature that automatically generates and maintains optimized delivery routes for drivers throughout their shift, with real-time recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, and vehicle-class enforcement.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: Route Planning (optimization algorithm, recalculation triggers), Fleet Management (driver shifts, vehicle licensing, zone assignment), Delivery Lifecyc

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain scenario carefully.

The feature is "Smart Route Optimization" for a logistics platform. Let me break down the bounded contexts and business rules.

Bounded contexts I can identify:
1. Route Planning / Optimization - route calculation, recalculation triggers
2. Fleet/Driver Management - vehicle classes, licensing, shift management
3. Delivery Management - delivery lifecycle, zones, status
4. Customer Notification - notification triggers, "next 3 stops" window

Let me identify business rules, invariants, acceptance criteria, and edge cases.

No contradictions here - the spec is reasonably clear. There are some ambiguities though:


- What happens when route is recalculated while driver is mid-delivery?
- What does "manually deviates" mean exactly? GPS-based? Driver-reported?
- "Zone" definition - how are zones defined? Geographic boundaries?
- What happens when a delivery fails and is removed from route?
- "Next 3 stops" - is this recalculated on each route change?
- What's the priority ordering for deliveries? Time windows? Distance?
- Can a driver reject an assigned route?

These are ambiguities - requirements that could be interpreted multiple ways. The escalation type should be `ambiguity`.

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform feature that automatically generates and maintains optimized delivery routes for drivers throughout their shift, with real-time recalculation triggers, customer proximity notifications, and vehicle-class enforcement.","bounded_context":"This feature spans at least four bounded contexts: Route Planning (optimization algorithm, recalculation triggers), Fleet Management (driver shifts, vehicle licensing, zone assignment), Delivery Lifecycle (delivery status, zone membership, failure handling), and Customer Notification (proximity-based alerts). The primary context under design is Route Planning, which consumes events from the other three.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize travel time/distance within their zone for a given shift."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area that scopes both driver assignment and delivery grouping. A driver operates within exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The act of recomputing the optimized route in response to a defined trigger event (new delivery, failed delivery, or manual deviation)."},{"term":"Manual Deviation","definition":"When a driver physically departs from the system-suggested route sequence, detected by the system (e.g., visiting stops out of order or traveling off-path)."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A sliding window of the three upcoming delivery stops on the driver's active route, used to trigger customer notifications."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of vehicle type (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight) that constrains which deliveries a driver may be assigned based on shipment requirements."},{"term":"Delivery Stop","definition":"A single delivery location on a route, with an estimated service time (default 4 minutes, overridable per zone)."},{"term":"Shift Start","definition":"The event marking a driver's availability, which triggers initial route assignment."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery attempt that was unsuccessful, requiring removal from the active route and triggering recalculation."},{"term":"Estimated Stop Time","definition":"The configurable time budget per delivery stop, defaulting to 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system must automatically compute and assign an optimized route from all pending deliveries in their zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy — the business could choose manual assignment or delayed assignment as alternatives. It is not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Within [SLA TBD] seconds of a driver starting their shift, a route containing all pending zone deliveries (compatible with the driver's vehicle class) is assigned to them."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A driver must never be assigned a delivery that exceeds their licensed vehicle class capability.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Assigning a motorcycle driver to a freight shipment is physically impossible and likely violates transport regulations. If this constraint is violated, the system is in a corrupt state — the delivery cannot be fulfilled and may create legal liability.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass <= driver.licensedVehicleClass. No route assignment or recalculation may produce a violation."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a new delivery is added to the driver's zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an optimization policy. The business could choose to batch recalculations or defer them. The system would not be corrupt without immediate recalculation.","testable_assertion":"When a new delivery enters a zone with an active driver route, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery within [SLA TBD]."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route must be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy for route efficiency. The system would not be corrupt if a failed delivery simply stayed on the route temporarily.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery status transitions to 'failed', it is removed from the active route and the remaining route is reoptimized."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The route must be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational optimization policy. A stale route is suboptimal but does not corrupt system state.","testable_assertion":"When the system detects that a driver has deviated from the planned route sequence, recalculation is triggered using the driver's current position as the new starting point."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Customers must be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's active route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification is a business policy, not a data integrity constraint. Missing a notification does not corrupt the domain.","testable_assertion":"When a route is calculated or recalculated, any customer whose delivery is now within the first 3 stops of the route (and was not previously notified) receives a notification."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated time per delivery stop is 4 minutes, overridable by the operations team at the zone level.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter, explicitly designed to be changed by operations staff.","testable_assertion":"Route time estimates use the zone-specific override if configured, otherwise default to 4 minutes per stop. Only the operations team role can set overrides."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their designated zone for the current shift.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries define the operational domain of a driver. Cross-zone assignment would violate the fundamental partitioning model — the system cannot guarantee route quality, SLA compliance, or regulatory adherence if zone boundaries are ignored.","testable_assertion":"Every delivery on a driver's route must have delivery.zone == driver.currentShift.zone. No route assignment or recalculation may assign cross-zone deliveries."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift in zone A with 10 pending deliveries in zone A","when":"The shift start event is processed","then":"The system assigns an optimized route containing all 10 deliveries, ordered to minimize travel time/distance, within the route calculation SLA"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver with an active route in zone A","when":"A new delivery is created in zone A","then":"The driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery, and any customers newly in the 'next 3 stops' window are notified"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver with an active route containing delivery D at stop 5","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"Delivery D is removed from the route, remaining stops are reoptimized, and the 'next 3 stops' window is re-evaluated for notifications"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver whose suggested next stop is location B","when":"The driver proceeds to location C (a later stop) instead","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A driver with a motorcycle license and an active route","when":"A freight-class delivery is added to their zone","then":"The freight delivery is NOT added to the motorcycle driver's route, even though it is in their zone"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer whose delivery is at position 4 on the route","when":"Route recalculation moves their delivery to position 3","then":"The customer receives a 'your delivery is approaching' notification"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer who was already notified (delivery was at position 2)","when":"Route recalculation keeps their delivery within the next 3 stops","then":"The customer is NOT notified again (no duplicate notifications)"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"Zone B has a configured stop time override of 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"Each delivery stop uses 6 minutes (not the default 4) for time estimation"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A driver in zone A with an active route","when":"A new delivery is created in zone B","then":"The delivery is NOT added to the zone A driver's route"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"Multiple drivers are active in zone A","when":"A new delivery arrives in zone A","then":"The system assigns the delivery to the most optimal driver and recalculates only the affected driver's route"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A driver starts their shift but there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","expected_behavior":"The driver is assigned an empty route. When the first delivery arrives in their zone, recalculation triggers and assigns it."},{"scenario":"All deliveries in a zone require a vehicle class higher than the only active driver's license","expected_behavior":"The driver receives an empty route. Deliveries remain unassigned/pending. The system should alert operations that unserviceable deliveries exist."},{"scenario":"A route recalculation is triggered while a previous recalculation is still in progress","expected_behavior":"The system must handle concurrent triggers gracefully — either queue recalculations or cancel-and-restart with the latest state. The route must never reflect stale data."},{"scenario":"A driver deviates from the route to the location of an upcoming stop (skip-ahead)","expected_behavior":"The system detects deviation, confirms the driver is at a valid delivery stop, marks it as the current stop, and recalculates the remaining route from that position."},{"scenario":"A customer's delivery oscillates in and out of the 'next 3 stops' window across multiple rapid recalculations","expected_behavior":"The customer receives at most one notification. Once notified, the flag is set and persists regardless of subsequent recalculations."},{"scenario":"A delivery is added to a zone with multiple active drivers","expected_behavior":"The optimization must decide which driver gets the delivery based on route efficiency, not arbitrary assignment. This implies a cross-route optimization or assignment heuristic."},{"scenario":"The operations team changes the zone stop time override mid-shift while drivers have active routes","expected_behavior":"Active routes should recalculate ETAs using the new stop time. Whether this triggers a full route reorder or only ETA updates needs clarification."},{"scenario":"A driver's shift ends but deliveries remain on their route","expected_behavior":"Undelivered stops must be returned to the pending pool and reassigned to the next available driver. They must not be silently dropped."},{"scenario":"A failed delivery is retried — does it re-enter the pending pool?","expected_behavior":"Business must define retry policy. If retried, the delivery re-enters the pending pool and triggers recalculation for an available driver. Retry count limits should apply."},{"scenario":"Driver completes all deliveries mid-shift and new deliveries arrive later","expected_behavior":"The driver should receive a recalculated route with the new deliveries, as they are still on shift and in-zone."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, increases deliveries per shift (throughput), and reduces overtime. Vehicle class enforcement avoids regulatory fines and failed delivery attempts. Accurate ETAs reduce missed-delivery costs.","operational":"Automatic route assignment eliminates manual dispatch overhead. Real-time recalculation keeps routes optimal as conditions change. Zone-level stop time overrides let operations fine-tune for local conditions (urban density, building access, etc.).","user":"Customer notifications at 'next 3 stops' improve delivery experience and reduce 'where is my package' inquiries. Drivers get clear, optimized routes reducing cognitive load and decision fatigue.","risk":"Route optimization algorithm quality is critical — a poor algorithm negates all benefits. Frequent recalculations could cause driver confusion or notification spam. Vehicle class enforcement failure creates legal/safety liability. Zone boundary accuracy is foundational — incorrect zones corrupt all routing.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Primary users. Route quality directly affects their workload, earnings (if performance-based), and job satisfaction. Excessive recalculations mid-drive could be disorienting or dangerous."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving visibility. Poor routing extends delivery windows and erodes trust."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration control. Reduced manual dispatch. Must monitor for unserviceable deliveries and driver-vehicle mismatches."},{"group":"Finance/Management","impact":"Expects measurable improvement in deliveries-per-shift, fuel costs, and on-time delivery rates as KPIs."},{"group":"Compliance/Legal","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement is a regulatory requirement. Audit trail of route assignments and vehicle class checks may be required."}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The spec clearly defines the core triggers (shift start, new delivery, failed delivery, deviation) and the vehicle class constraint. However, multiple operational details are described vaguely or not at all: how deviation is detected, how multi-driver assignment works, what optimization objective is used, how zone boundaries are defined, and what happens at shift boundaries. These ambiguities affect the ability to build a correct implementation without making assumptions.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class enforcement is a hard invariant — clearly stated and unambiguous","Three recalculation triggers are well-defined events","Customer notification at 'next 3 stops' is a clear threshold","Default stop time of 4 minutes with zone-level override is explicit"],"low_confidence_areas":["How 'manual deviation' is detected — GPS threshold? Sequence violation? Driver-reported?","Multi-driver zone assignment strategy — how does the system choose which driver gets a new delivery?","Route optimization objective — minimize distance? Time? Balance load across drivers?","Shift lifecycle — what happens to route when shift ends, or during break?","Failed delivery retry policy — does it re-enter the pool? How many retries?","Zone definition and management — how are zones created, bounded, and updated?"],"assumptions":["A driver operates in exactly one zone per shift","Vehicle class is a hierarchical capability (van driver can handle motorcycle-class deliveries)","'Next 3 stops' notification is sent at most once per customer per delivery","Route recalculation replaces the entire remaining route, not just inserts/removes a single stop","The system has real-time or near-real-time driver location data for deviation detection"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Manual deviation' detection mechanism is unspecified. This could mean GPS-based off-path detection, visiting stops out of order, or driver self-reporting. Each interpretation produces fundamentally different system requirements (real-time GPS tracking vs. check-in sequence monitoring vs. UI interaction).","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Engineering","Operations Team"],"options":["GPS geofence-based: trigger when driver is >X meters from expected route for >Y seconds","Sequence-based: trigger when driver checks in at a stop that is not the next planned stop","Driver-initiated: driver taps 'reroute' in their app"],"recommendation":"Sequence-based detection is the most pragmatic starting point — it requires no continuous GPS processing and aligns with the delivery check-in flow. GPS-based can be layered on later."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-driver assignment within a zone is not addressed. When a new delivery enters a zone with multiple active drivers, the spec says 'recalculate the route' but does not specify WHICH driver's route, or whether cross-driver optimization occurs.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations Team","Customers"],"options":["Nearest-driver: assign to driver currently closest to the new delivery","Least-loaded: assign to driver with fewest remaining stops","Global reoptimization: reoptimize all drivers' routes in the zone simultaneously"],"recommendation":"Least-loaded with distance tiebreaker is the simplest fair heuristic. Global reoptimization is ideal but significantly more complex — recommend as a Phase 2 enhancement."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Route optimization objective function is unspecified. 'Optimized route' could mean minimize total distance, minimize total time, minimize latest delivery time, balance workload across drivers, or prioritize time-sensitive deliveries.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Customers","Finance/Management"],"options":["Minimize total travel distance (fuel-efficient)","Minimize total route time including stop times (throughput-maximizing)","Minimize maximum individual delivery ETA (customer-fairness-maximizing)"],"recommendation":"Minimize total route time is the most common industry default as it directly maximizes throughput. Customer-facing SLA constraints should be layered on as hard constraints, not the objective function."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
